perturbation.original_text: Each party shall exercise such care in the protection of the confidential information of the other as they exercise in the protection of confidential information of their own.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall exercise such care in the protection of the confidential information of the other as they exercise in the protection of non-confidential information of their own.
perturbation.explanation: This is an in-text contradiction. The original states both parties shall take the same care as they would in protecting their own confidential information. The revision states both parties shall exercise the same care in protecting confidential information of the other as they would in protecting their own non-confidential information. This means both parties don't need to protect anything, which is the opposite of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix and Company agree that they shall hold in confidence and shall not disclose any Confidential Information (as defined in Paragraph 2 below) without the prior written authorization from any employee of the party to whom the information belongs, or use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than that contemplated by this Agreement. This obligation, however, shall not extend to any of the following: A. Confidential Information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain if authorized by a designated employee; B. Confidential Information which after generation or disclosure is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the disclosing party (but only after and to the extent that it is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain); C. Confidential Information which either party can show was in its possession at the time of generation or disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the other party or from a third party under obligation of confidence; D. Confidential Information which was received after the time of generation or disclosure hereunder, from a third party who did not require that party to hold it in confidence and who did not acquire it, directly or indirectly, form the other party under an obligation of confidence; and <PAGE> 2 E. Confidential Information which Phoenix and Company can show was developed independently without benefit of, or based on information generated hereunder or made available by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that both parties shall exercise the same care as they would in protecting their own confidential information, whereas the changed text states that both parties shall exercise the same care in protecting confidential information of the other as they would in protecting their own non-confidential information. This implies a lower or non-existent standard of care, contradicting the contradicted text which mandates holding confidential information in confidence and restricting its disclosure or use. The change introduces an inconsistency in the level of protection afforded to confidential information, directly conflicting with the explicit confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall NOT be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause binds heirs, assigns, and successors to the agreement's rights and obligations. The changed clause explicitly states that they are NOT bound. This directly contradicts the original intent of ensuring the agreement's continued effect even if the original parties are no longer directly involved, creating significant legal uncertainty about the agreement's long-term enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall NOT be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the rights and obligations of the agreement shall NOT be binding upon heirs, assigns, and successors of Phoenix and Company. The contradicted text states the same thing: 'Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall NOT be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company.' Although both sentences are the same, the changed text directly contradicts the original text 'Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company' which creates a contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: At the termination of this examination, both parties agree to return to each other all of the documents and other information provided in connection with this examination, and all copies thereof, as soon as requested by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: At the termination of this examination, both parties agree to destroy all of the documents and other information provided in connection with this examination, and all copies thereof, as soon as requested by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, parties had to *return* all confidential material. Now, they must *destroy* it. This creates a direct contradiction in terms of what to do with the confidential information after the examination ends, leading to potential legal disputes. This contradiction creates an inconsistency that can be problematic during the end of the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the termination of this examination, both parties agree to destroy all of the documents and other information provided in connection with this examination, and all copies thereof, as soon as requested by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original requirement to *return* confidential materials to requiring parties to *destroy* them. The contradicted text also states that the parties must *destroy* the confidential materials. This creates a direct contradiction because it means the parties are destroying the materials rather than returning them. The explanation given also explains this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Phoenix and Company warrant that they have the unqualified right to disclose fully the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Phoenix and Company warrant that they have the unqualified right to disclose fully the Non-Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that both parties have the unqualified right to disclose confidential information. The new clause states that both parties have the right to disclose Non-Confidential Information. The opposite of this is that they don't have the right to disclose confidential information. This is an in-text contradiction because the whole document is about confidential information and why the parties are required to protect the others.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix and Company agree that they shall hold in confidence and shall not disclose any Confidential Information (as defined in Paragraph 2 below) without the prior written authorization from any employee of the party to whom the information belongs, or use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than that contemplated by this Agreement. This obligation, however, shall not extend to any of the following: A. Confidential Information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain if authorized by a designated employee; B. Confidential Information which after generation or disclosure is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the disclosing party (but only after and to the extent that it is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain); C. Confidential Information which either party can show was in its possession at the time of generation or disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the other party or from a third party under obligation of confidence; D. Confidential Information which was received after the time of generation or disclosure hereunder, from a third party who did not require that party to hold it in confidence and who did not acquire it, directly or indirectly, form the other party under an obligation of confidence; and <PAGE> 2 E. Confidential Information which Phoenix and Company can show was developed independently without benefit of, or based on information generated hereunder or made available by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Phoenix and Company have the right to disclose confidential information, aligning with the contradicted text, which obligates them to protect it. The changed text modifies this to state that they have the right to disclose non-confidential information. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text because the document primarily addresses confidential information. By implication, if they have the right to disclose non-confidential information only, it suggests they do *not* have the right to disclose confidential information, directly contradicting the contradicted text where they agree to hold Confidential Information in confidence. This aligns with the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition, specifically when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
